utf.c

00025 
00026 /*=========================================================================
00027  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00028  * SUBSYSTEM: Unicode translators.
00029  * FILE:      utf.c
00030  * OVERVIEW:  Routines for Unicode -> UTF and UTF -> unicode translators.
00031  *
00032  * This file implements the unicode -> UTF and UTF -> unicode translators
00033  * needed by the various parts of the compiler and interpreter.
00034  *
00035  * UTF strings are streams of bytes, in which unicode characters are encoded
00036  * as follows:
00037  *       Unicode                  UTF
00038  *       00000000 0jklmnop       0jklmnop
00039  *       00000fgh ijklmnop       110fghij 10klmnop
00040  *       abcdefgh ijklmnop       1110abcd 10efghij 10klmnop
00041  *
00042  * unicode bytes with 7 or fewer significant bits MUST be converted using the
00043  * first format.  bytes with 11 or fewer bits MUST be converted using the
00044  * second format.
00045  *
00046  * In JAVA/JAVAC, we deviate slightly from the above.
00047  *    1) The null unicode character is represented using the 2-byte format
00048  *    2)  All UTF strings are null-terminated.
00049  * In this way, we do not need to separately maintain a length field for the
00050  * UTF string.
00051  *
00052  * Given a unicode string and its length, convert it to a utf string.  But
00053  * the result into the given buffer, whose length is buflength.  The utf
00054  * string should include a null terminator.
00055  *
00056  * If both buffer and buflength are 0, then malloc an appropriately sized
00057  * buffer for the result.
00058  *
00059  *=======================================================================*/
00060 
00061 /*=========================================================================
00062  * Include files
00063  *=======================================================================*/
00064 
00065 #include "wrappers.h"
00066 
00067 #include "oobj.h"
00068 #include "utf.h"
00069 #include "sys_api.h"
00070 
00071 char *unicode2utf(unicode *unistring, int length, char *buffer, int buflength)
00072 {
00073     int i;
00074     unicode *uniptr;
00075     char *bufptr;
00076     unsigned bufleft;
00077 
00078     if ((buffer == 0) && (buflength == 0)) {
00079     buflength = unicode2utfstrlen(unistring, length);
00080     if ((buffer = (char *) sysMalloc(buflength)) == 0)
00081         return 0;
00082     }
00083 
00084     bufleft = buflength - 1; /* take note of null now! */
00085 
00086     for(i = length, uniptr = unistring, bufptr = buffer; --i >= 0; uniptr++) {
00087     unicode ch = *uniptr;
00088     if ((ch != 0) && (ch <=0x7f)) {
00089         if ((int)(--bufleft) < 0)    /* no space for character */
00090             break;
00091         *bufptr++ = (char)ch;
00092     } else if (ch <= 0x7FF) {
00093         /* 11 bits or less. */
00094         unsigned char high_five = ch >> 6;
00095         unsigned char low_six = ch & 0x3F;
00096         if ((int)(bufleft -= 2) < 0) /* no space for character */
00097             break;
00098         *bufptr++ = high_five | 0xC0; /* 110xxxxx */
00099         *bufptr++ = low_six | 0x80;      /* 10xxxxxx */
00100     } else {
00101         /* possibly full 16 bits. */
00102         char high_four = ch >> 12;
00103         char mid_six = (ch >> 6) & 0x3F;
00104         char low_six = ch & 0x3f;
00105         if ((int)(bufleft -= 3) < 0) /* no space for character */
00106             break;
00107         *bufptr++ = high_four | 0xE0; /* 1110xxxx */
00108         *bufptr++ = mid_six | 0x80;   /* 10xxxxxx */
00109         *bufptr++ = low_six | 0x80;   /* 10xxxxxx*/
00110     }
00111     }
00112     *bufptr = 0;
00113     return buffer;
00114 }
00115 
00116 /* Return the number of characters that would be needed to hold the unicode
00117  * string in utf.  This INCLUDES the NULL!
00118  */
00119 int unicode2utfstrlen(unicode *unistring, int unilength)
00120 {
00121     int result_length = 1;
00122 
00123     for (; unilength > 0; unistring++, unilength--) {
00124     unicode ch = *unistring;
00125     if ((ch != 0) && (ch <= 0x7f)) /* 1 byte */
00126         result_length++;
00127     else if (ch <= 0x7FF)
00128         result_length += 2;    /* 2 byte character */
00129     else
00130         result_length += 3;    /* 3 byte character */
00131     }
00132     return result_length;
00133 }
00134 
00135 /* Give the number of unicode characters in a utf string */
00136 int utfstrlen(char *utfstring)
00137 {
00138     int length;
00139     for (length = 0; *utfstring != 0; length++)
00140         next_utf2unicode(&utfstring);
00141     return length;
00142 }
00143 
00144 /* Convert a utfstring to unicode in the buffer provided.  Put at most
00145  * max_length characters into the buffer.  Whether or not we actually overflow
00146  * the space, indicate the actual unicode length.
00147  *
00148  * Whether or not we overflow the space, return the actual number of
00149  * characters that we used.
00150  */
00151 
00152 void
00153 utf2unicode(char *utfstring, unicode *unistring,
00154         int max_length, int *lengthp)
00155 {
00156     int length_remaining = max_length;
00157 
00158     while (length_remaining > 0 && *utfstring != 0) {
00159     *unistring++ = next_utf2unicode(&utfstring);
00160     length_remaining--;
00161     }
00162 
00163     if (length_remaining == 0) {
00164     *lengthp = max_length + utfstrlen(utfstring);
00165     } else {
00166     *lengthp = max_length - length_remaining;
00167     }
00168 }
00169 
00170 bool is_simple_utf(char *utfstring)
00171 {
00172     unsigned char *ptr;
00173     for (ptr = (unsigned char *)utfstring; *ptr != 0; ptr++) {
00174     if (*ptr > 0x80) return false;
00175     }
00176     return true;
00177 }
00178 
00179 unicode next_utf2unicode(char **utfstring_ptr) {
00180     unsigned char *ptr = (unsigned char *)(*utfstring_ptr);
00181     unsigned char ch, ch2, ch3;
00182     int length = 1;        /* default length */
00183     unicode result = 0x80;    /* default bad result; */
00184     switch ((ch = ptr[0]) >> 4) {
00185         default:
00186         result = ch;
00187         break;
00188 
00189     case 0x8: case 0x9: case 0xA: case 0xB: case 0xF:
00190         /* Shouldn't happen. */
00191         break;
00192 
00193     case 0xC: case 0xD:
00194         /* 110xxxxx  10xxxxxx */
00195         if (((ch2 = ptr[1]) & 0xC0) == 0x80) {
00196         unsigned char high_five = ch & 0x1F;
00197         unsigned char low_six = ch2 & 0x3F;
00198         result = (high_five << 6) + low_six;
00199         length = 2;
00200         }
00201         break;
00202 
00203     case 0xE:
00204         /* 1110xxxx 10xxxxxx 10xxxxxx */
00205         if (((ch2 = ptr[1]) & 0xC0) == 0x80) {
00206         if (((ch3 = ptr[2]) & 0xC0) == 0x80) {
00207             unsigned char high_four = ch & 0x0f;
00208             unsigned char mid_six = ch2 & 0x3f;
00209             unsigned char low_six = ch3 & 0x3f;
00210             result = (((high_four << 6) + mid_six) << 6) + low_six;
00211             length = 3;
00212         } else {
00213             length = 2;
00214         }
00215         }
00216         break;
00217     } /* end of switch */
00218 
00219     *utfstring_ptr = (char *)(ptr + length);
00220     return result;
00221 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:28 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
